---
title: "Project Team"
description-meta: "Research team and advisory group for the MMLoS project"
---

# Project Team

The MMLoS project is a collaboration between two universities and benefits from an expert advisory group.

## Core Team

| Role | Name | Affiliation | Expertise |
|------|------|-------------|-----------|
| **Principal Investigator** | Dr Tom Cohen | University of Westminster · Active Travel Academy | Transport policy, multi-modal level of service, street design |
| **Co-Investigator** | Prof Robin Lovelace | University of Leeds · Institute for Transport Studies | Transport data science, GIS, active travel modelling, open-source tools |
| **Research Assistant** | [TBD – Westminster student] | University of Westminster | Literature review, data collation, analysis |
| **Research Software Engineer** | [To be recruited] | — | Rust, Python, web development, tool implementation |
| **Research Fellow** | [To be recruited] | — | Transport planning, stakeholder engagement, case-study coordination |

## Subcontractors

### IFP-r (International Federation of Pedestrians – Research Branch)

IFP-r will conduct **3D LiDAR scanning** at two case-study locations, producing high-resolution point clouds that capture:

- Pavement geometry and surface quality
- Kerb heights, dropped kerbs, tactile paving
- Lane widths, carriageway conditions
- Obstructions, foliage overhang, pinch points

This data will enable precise measurement of infrastructure attributes relevant to pedestrian and wheelchair user Level of Service.

**IFP-r Team:**
- **Hendrik van Waeg** – Research Engineer
- **Alan Luis Lacey Raposo** – Project Coordinator

IFP-r has prior experience in European Horizon projects (REALLOCATE, JUST STREETS) and has collected LiDAR data across cities including Lyon, Gothenburg, Barcelona, Bologna, and Utrecht.

## Design Advisory Group (DAG)

The DAG provides expert guidance on methodological and philosophical questions fundamental to the MMLoS tool's operation. Members serve pro bono.

| Name | Affiliation | Expertise |
|------|-------------|-----------|
| **Rachel Aldred** | University of Westminster · Active Travel Academy | Active travel, road safety, cycling policy, Propensity to Cycle Tool |
| **Peter White** | University of Westminster (Emeritus) | Public transport, bus service planning, transport modelling |
| **Jamie Furlong** | University of Westminster · Active Travel Academy | Quantitative methods, statistics, cycleability measures |

## Steering Group

The project Steering Group includes representatives from:

- **Active Travel England** (client)
- **Local authority partners** — Essex County Council, Transport for West Midlands, Leeds City Council
- **Academic partners** — University of Westminster, University of Leeds

The Steering Group meets approximately every two months to review progress, advise on direction, and help select case-study locations.

---

*Interested in joining the team or collaborating? Contact [Tom Cohen](mailto:t.cohen@westminster.ac.uk) or [Robin Lovelace](mailto:r.lovelace@leeds.ac.uk).*
